name: Seattle
description: Seattle, Washington — history, landmarks, sports, and culture

questions.q: Seattle is the largest city in which U.S. state?

source.notes: Olympia is the state capital; Seattle is by far the largest city.

questions.q: Which observation tower built for the 1962 World's Fair is Seattle's most iconic landmark?

source.notes: 605 feet tall; topped with a revolving restaurant.

questions.q: What is Seattle's most famous nickname?

source.notes: Coined in a 1981 naming contest for its evergreen surroundings.

questions.q: Which coffee company, founded in 1971 at Pike Place Market, is headquartered in Seattle?

source.notes: Named after the first mate in Moby-Dick.

questions.q: Which major online retailer has its corporate headquarters in Seattle's Denny Triangle?

source.notes: Including the distinctive 'Spheres' biodome complex.

questions.q: Seattle's NFL team won Super Bowl XLVIII in 2014. What are they called?

source.notes: Defeated the Denver Broncos 43-8.

questions.q: Which imposing stratovolcano is visible to the south of Seattle on clear days?

source.notes: 14,411 feet tall; the highest peak in the Cascade Range.

questions.q: In what year was Seattle officially founded?

source.notes: Founding date of the Denny Party's landing at Alki Point.

questions.q: Seattle is named after which Native American leader?

source.notes: Leader of the Suquamish and Duwamish tribes.

questions.q: Which devastating 1889 event destroyed much of downtown Seattle and spurred major rebuilding?

source.notes: Destroyed around 25 city blocks on June 6, 1889.

questions.q: Which bustling Seattle market, dating from 1907, is famous for its fish-throwing vendors?

source.notes: Includes the first Starbucks storefront.

questions.q: Which major aerospace company, founded 1916, has its commercial-airplanes headquarters in the Seattle area?

source.notes: Corporate HQ moved to Chicago (2001) and then Arlington, VA (2022).

questions.q: Which late-1980s/1990s music genre associated with Nirvana, Pearl Jam, and Soundgarden is rooted in Seattle?

source.notes: Alice in Chains is another major Seattle grunge band.

questions.q: Seattle is the seat of which Washington state county?

source.notes: Originally named for Vice President William Rufus King.

questions.q: Which 1999 international conference held in Seattle sparked mass protests over globalization?

source.notes: Now known simply as 'the Battle in Seattle.'

questions.q: Which Seattle-based NHL franchise began play in 2021?

source.notes: Plays at Climate Pledge Arena.

questions.q: The Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, one of the world's largest private charitable foundations, is based in which city?

source.notes: Microsoft itself is headquartered in nearby Redmond.

questions.q: Which Seattle neighborhood is historically associated with vibrant LGBTQ+ culture?

source.notes: Home of Seattle's annual Pride parade.

questions.q: The 1962 Seattle World's Fair was officially known by what name?

source.notes: Gave Seattle the Space Needle and Monorail.

questions.q: Seattle is traditionally said to be built on approximately how many hills?

source.notes: Capitol Hill, First Hill, Queen Anne, Beacon Hill, Magnolia, West Seattle, Denny Hill.

questions.q: In which year did Microsoft relocate its headquarters to the Seattle region (Bellevue, later Redmond)?

source.notes: Moved from Albuquerque, NM.

questions.q: The February 2001 Nisqually earthquake that shook Seattle had what approximate magnitude?

source.notes: Deep intraslab earthquake beneath Puget Sound.
